what_it_does: A single-file CSS stylesheet for the AtomiCity application shell and its feature faces, defining layout (top bar, tree rail, detail pane, fixed Vi console panel, command bar), component styling, Ackit primitives, view transitions, and reduced-motion handling. It attempts to express every visual value through ConceptV design tokens rather than raw literals.
observations: Heavy use of CSS custom properties (tokens) for color, spacing, radius, shadow, easing, and typography, with frequent color-mix() for transparency.
Defines a fixed application shell: .ac-top, .ac-rail, .ac-detail, and a slide-out .ac-vi-panel plus a fixed .ac-vi-fab.
Includes a command palette overlay (.ac-cmd-back / .ac-cmd) and an inspect mode triggered by :root[data-mode='inspect'].
Provides many component namespaces in one file: .ac-*, .k-*, .fz2-*, .ig-*, .hm-*, .ex2-*, .ex3-*, .vo-*, .fzp-*.
Uses SVG noise and CSS gradients to implement grain, blueprint grid, and hatched baseline motifs.
Contains view-transition styling for .ac-detail-inner with custom enter/exit keyframes and a reduced-motion media query.
Ackit primitives (.k-state, .k-tile, .k-field, .k-sheet, .k-ctl-*) include animated gem/check/ring state indicators.
Some raw hex/rgba literals remain despite the stated token-only contract (e.g., #fff, rgba(255,255,255,.06), #1F1A12, #b3261e).
standouts: The strict token-driven styling contract, where nearly every value is a var() or color-mix() expression.
A full Vi console UI expressed in CSS: floating FAB, slide-out panel, message bubbles, composer, and suggestion pills.
View-transition choreography scoped to the detail pane (::view-transition-old/new(ac-detail)).
Multi-face component styling collapsed into one stylesheet (foundations, ingest, home, explore, voice, plus preview card patterns).
conventions: Follows namespaced class prefixes (.ac-, .k-, .fz2-, etc.) and token-based spacing/radius/shadow/easing variables.
Uses var(--prop, fallback) for resilience and color-mix(in srgb, ...) for translucent surfaces.
Breaks its own no-raw-literals rule in several places: #fff backgrounds, rgba(255,255,255,...) top-bar modes, #1F1A12 ink fallback, #b3261e error fallback, and a hardcoded #fff iframe background.
Layout constants (rail 326px, Vi panel 392px, top 60px) are centralized in :root.
concerns: Large monolithic file mixing shell, primitives, and many feature faces, which may make targeted maintenance hard.
Several animations and properties depend on external ConceptV tokens not defined here (e.g., --font-display, --ease-emphasized, --move-sm, --glow-active).
View-transition keyframes reference --move-sm and --move-xs that are absent from this file.
Reduced-motion override only targets a few selectors with !important, leaving many other transitions/animations unguarded.
Raw color literals and opacity hacks appear despite the explicit token-only claim.
notes: A token-ambitious, single-file styling layer for AtomiCity that covers the app shell, Vi assistant UI, Ackit primitives, and multiple feature faces, but still carries some raw literals and external-token dependencies.
questions: Where are the ConceptV tokens (fonts, colors, easings, grain/blueprint variables) actually defined?
How is :root[data-mode='inspect'] toggled, and what JS coordinates the .ac-rail pointer-events change?
Do the view-transition names and --move-* variables rely on a separate app.css or JS view-transition API?
What runtime components map to the .fz2, .ig, .hm, .ex2/.ex3, and .vo namespaces?
Are the .k-* Ackit primitives intended to be reused outside AtomiCity, and where is their markup/SVG defined?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a ConceptV-token-only stylesheet for AtomiCity; actual content largely matches but includes raw color literals, hardcoded values, and app-specific component styling that goes beyond pure foundational tokens.
apparent_themes: Warm gold/bronze palette with grain and blueprint texture
Persistent AI assistant (Vi) embedded in the UI shell
Motion as a first-class material (easing, springs, view transitions)
Accessibility consideration via reduced-motion media query
intention_signals: The file is trying to make AtomiCity feel like a coherent, tactile, design-system-native application where every visual choice flows from ConceptV tokens, while embedding a persistent Vi assistant and supporting mode-driven inspection and smooth view transitions.
novelty: Collapsing a full app shell, a persistent AI assistant panel, primitive state-machine components, multiple feature-face designs, and view-transition choreography into one CSS file while attempting a strict token-only styling contract.
contribution: A comprehensive, token-oriented visual layer that defines the AtomiCity application shell, Vi console layout, warm/gold palette expression, grain+blueprint texture motifs, Ackit primitive states, and detail-pane view-transition choreography — material that a fused system would keep as the app's styling vocabulary.
summary_for_embedding: A monolithic CSS stylesheet for the AtomiCity app shell and feature faces, styling the top bar, rail, detail pane, Vi console, command bar, Ackit primitives, and multiple views using ConceptV tokens, color-mix, grain/blueprint motifs, and view transitions.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, token-driven styling source for AtomiCity that expresses the ConceptV design system, embeds the Vi assistant UI, and unifies shell and feature-face components.
